title: ConfigMap and Secret in a Pod
description: Write a ConfigMap, a Secret, and a Pod that consumes both as environment variables.
starterCode:
# Write three Kubernetes manifests separated by ---:
# 1. ConfigMap named 'app-config' with:
#    - APP_ENV: production
#    - LOG_LEVEL: warn
# 2. Secret named 'app-secrets' type: Opaque with stringData:
#    - DB_PASSWORD: SuperSecret123
# 3. Pod named 'app-pod' that:
#    - Loads ConfigMap as envFrom
#    - Loads DB_PASSWORD from Secret using env[].valueFrom.secretKeyRef

---
# ConfigMap

---
# Secret

---
# Pod
solutionCode:
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: app-config
data:
  APP_ENV: production
  LOG_LEVEL: warn
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: app-secrets
type: Opaque
stringData:
  DB_PASSWORD: SuperSecret123
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: app-pod
spec:
  containers:
    - name: app
      image: busybox:1.36
      command: ["sh", "-c", "env && sleep 3600"]
      envFrom:
        - configMapRef:
            name: app-config
      env:
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: app-secrets
              key: DB_PASSWORD
hints: Use stringData in Secrets to avoid base64 encoding manually
envFrom loads all ConfigMap keys as environment variables
secretKeyRef requires both name (secret name) and key (key in secret)
Use --- to separate multiple manifests in one file

validationRules.errorMessage: Use envFrom to load ConfigMap into Pod
Use secretKeyRef to load Secret value into env var
YAML is invalid — check --- separators and indentation

simulatedOutput.success:
configmap/app-config created
secret/app-secrets created
pod/app-pod created

Pod environment variables:
  APP_ENV=production (from ConfigMap)
  LOG_LEVEL=warn (from ConfigMap)
  DB_PASSWORD=****** (from Secret, masked)
simulatedOutput.failure: Validation failed. Check ConfigMap data, Secret stringData, envFrom configMapRef, and env secretKeyRef.
